description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular, single-story structure with a prominent gable roof clad in red shingles. The exterior walls are constructed from horizontal wooden planks, giving it a rustic and functional appearance. The front facade features a large double wooden door on the right, suitable for livestock or equipment access, and an open bay on the left supported by sturdy wooden posts. The sides and rear of the building are solid, with no visible windows or additional openings, emphasizing security and containment. The roof overhangs slightly, providing some shelter at the entrance and along the open bay. The overall design is practical, with minimal decorative elements, focusing on utility and durability.
description: A rectangular wooden cowshed with a red gable roof, double wooden doors, and an open bay supported by posts. The structure is rustic and utilitarian, with solid plank walls and no visible windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the double wooden doors on the right and an open bay on the left supported by wooden posts.
description_view_1: Gable end view displaying the solid wooden plank wall with no doors or windows, and the full slope of the red roof.
description_view_2: Side view revealing the long, uninterrupted wooden plank wall beneath the overhanging red roof, with no openings.
description_view_3: Opposite gable end view, similar to view 1, showing a solid wooden wall and the roof's peak.
attribute_door_style: double wooden plank doors with visible hinges
